message:
refactor(sync): factor shared JSONL temp-file setup/persist into helpers

write_jsonl_lines_atomically and write_existing_jsonl_replacements_atomically
(the new scan-rewrite fast path from 281269e) duplicated the same
~60 lines of temp-file prep + sync + durable-rename scaffolding
around different writer bodies. Pull that scaffolding into four
named helpers + one typed carrier so the two write paths focus on
"what to write" instead of "how to atomically swap it in".

New struct:

- JsonlTempOutput { temp_path: PathBuf, temp_guard: TempFileGuard,
  writer: BufWriter<File> } — bundles the three pieces each write
  path needs to hand back to the persist step. Replaces the
  previous per-function local variables.

New helpers:

- prepare_jsonl_temp_output(output_path, config) → JsonlTempOutput
  Runs validate_sync_path_with_external +
  history::backup_before_export (when config.beads_dir is set),
  mkdir -p the parent, computes export_temp_path + validates it
  via validate_temp_file_path, opens the temp file with
  create_new(true) (preserving the "temp already exists" → Config
  error mapping), wraps it in a TempFileGuard, applies
  restrictive permissions, and returns the bundle. Single source
  of truth for how temp files come into existence on the sync
  path.

- persist_jsonl_temp_output(temp_output, output_path, config):
  destructures the carrier, calls sync_jsonl_writer then
  rename_jsonl_temp_output. Used by
  write_existing_jsonl_replacements_atomically which hashes + counts
  inline and just needs a one-shot "close + swap" at the end.

- rename_jsonl_temp_output(temp_path, temp_guard, output_path,
  config): runs the two require_safe_sync_overwrite_path checks
  (temp → beads_dir safe, output → beads_dir safe) gated on
  config.beads_dir, then crate::util::durable_rename and
  temp_guard.persist(). write_jsonl_lines_atomically calls this
  one DIRECTLY rather than going through persist_jsonl_temp_output
  because it needs to run a count_issues_in_jsonl(&temp_path)
  invariant check BETWEEN sync and rename — if the count-mismatch
  branch fires, the temp guard must still drop/delete the partial
  temp file.

- sync_jsonl_writer(writer: BufWriter<File>): writer.flush +
  into_inner (with the existing IntoInnerError → BeadsError::Io
  mapping) + sync_all. Called from both persist_jsonl_temp_output
  and inline from write_jsonl_lines_atomically for the
  count-check-between-sync-and-rename case.

- absolute_or_current_dir_join(path): small utility that replaces
  the inline "if path.is_absolute() { p } else if let Ok(cwd) =
  current_dir() { cwd.join(p) } else { p }" expression that both
  write paths grew a copy of for computing the backup_before_export
  argument. Now used once inside prepare_jsonl_temp_output.

Behaviour is preserved end-to-end. Every validation check, error
mapping, permission setting, sync call, and rename ordering is
present in the new helpers in the same order as before; the two
write paths are mechanically shorter because all of this is
factored out. sync/mod.rs shrinks by ~60 net lines despite the new
helpers, because each helper's body replaces two near-identical
inline blocks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
